stemHtml: You are the commander of a commercial flight and are informed that an able-bodied person is sitting in an emergency exit row while holding a dog. What is the appropriate course of action to take in this situation?

options.a: No action is required, as this is allowed according to EASA Air-OPS regulations.
options.b: Place the dog on a different seat away from the emergency exit.
options.c: The able-bodied person and the dog must be reseated away from the emergency exit.
options.d: According to EASA Air-OPS, dogs are not allowed in the passenger cabin. The dog must be placed in a box and transported in the hold bay.
